name: end
detailed_description: End the current session. Call this when you have completed all pending work,
have no more actions to take, or have been explicitly asked to stop. Must be
called alone — no other tool calls in the same turn.

name: send_message
description: Send message to agent or user. target_agent_id must be a UUID, not a display name.
detailed_description: Send a direct message to another agent or the user. Use for 1-on-1
communication: task assignments, status updates, questions, or delivering
results. The target_agent_id must be a valid UUID — use list_agents to discover
agent IDs. To message the human user, use the user's agent UUID (provided in
your context).

target_agent_id.description: Recipient UUID (agent id). Never pass display names here.

name: broadcast_message
detailed_description: Send a message visible to all agents in the project. Use for announcements,
status broadcasts, or information that every team member needs to see. Prefer
send_message for targeted communication to avoid noise.

name: update_memory
detailed_description: Overwrite your persistent working memory. Memory survives across sessions and
context window resets. Store: current goals, progress notes, key decisions,
blockers, or any state you need to recall later. The entire memory is replaced
on each call, so always include everything you want to keep.

name: read_history
detailed_description: Read your past messages from previous sessions. Useful for recovering context
after a restart, reviewing past decisions, or checking what you've communicated.
Results are ordered newest-first. Use limit/offset for pagination and session_id
to filter to a specific session.

name: sleep
detailed_description: Pause execution for a specified number of seconds (0–3600). Use when waiting for
an external process to complete, throttling operations, or when a deliberate
delay is needed before checking status.

name: list_tasks
detailed_description: List all tasks on the project kanban board. Optionally filter by status
(backlog, in_progress, review, done, aborted). Use to get an overview of project
progress, find unassigned work, or check what tasks exist.

name: get_task_details
detailed_description: Get full details of a specific task by its UUID, including title, description,
status, assigned agent, git branch, and PR URL. Use when you need to understand
a task's full scope before starting work, or to check a task's current state.

name: execute_command
description: Execute shell command in your sandbox container.
detailed_description: *** PRIMARY TOOL — use creatively and boldly for any executable task. ***
Runs a shell command inside your dedicated sandbox container (Ubuntu 22.04, full root access).
This is your go-to tool for: running code, file operations, installing packages,
running tests, git operations, HTTP requests (curl/wget), and more.
Key behaviors:
- The sandbox persists across all calls — files, packages, env vars carry over.
- Chain commands with && or write shell scripts for multi-step workflows.
- If a command fails, read stderr carefully, fix the issue, and retry.
- You can install any tool (apt-get / pip / npm); installs persist for the session.
- Call sandbox_start_session() first if you get a 'sandbox not ready' error.

name: start_sandbox
detailed_description: Ensures your sandbox container is running. Idempotent — safe to call multiple times.
Prefer calling sandbox_start_session() directly.

name: sandbox_start_session
description: Ensure sandbox container is running for this session.
detailed_description: Allocate and start your dedicated sandbox container. Call this at the beginning
of any session that requires execute_command, screenshot, or display access.
Idempotent — safe to call multiple times. Returns sandbox status and ID.

name: sandbox_end_session
description: Release sandbox back to the pool when done.
detailed_description: Release your sandbox container back to the shared pool. The container is reset
and made available for other agents. Your /workspace files are preserved in the
named volume and will be remounted if you request a sandbox again. Call when you
have finished all sandbox work for the current session.

name: sandbox_health
description: Check sandbox container health and uptime.
detailed_description: Ping your sandbox container's health endpoint and return its current status,
uptime in seconds, and virtual display info. Use to verify the sandbox is
responsive before starting intensive work or after a suspected timeout.

name: sandbox_screenshot
description: Take a screenshot of the sandbox display.
detailed_description: Capture the current state of the sandbox virtual display (Xvfb :99) as a JPEG
image. The result is returned as a base64-encoded string. Use to observe the
current state of any GUI application running in the sandbox.

name: sandbox_mouse_move
description: Move mouse cursor to (x, y) in sandbox display.
detailed_description: Move the mouse cursor to the specified coordinates in the sandbox virtual display.
Coordinates are in pixels (0,0 = top-left of 1024x768 display).

name: sandbox_mouse_location
description: Get current mouse cursor position in sandbox.
detailed_description: Return the current {x, y} coordinates of the mouse cursor in the sandbox virtual
display. Useful for verifying mouse position after moves.

name: sandbox_keyboard_press
description: Send key press or type text in sandbox.
detailed_description: Send keyboard input to the sandbox. Use 'keys' for special key combos (e.g.
'ctrl+c', 'Return', 'Escape', 'alt+F4') or 'text' to type a string of
characters. Exactly one of 'keys' or 'text' must be provided.

text.description: Raw text to type into the focused window

name: sandbox_record_screen
detailed_description: Start an ffmpeg screen recording of the sandbox virtual display at 2 fps.
Recording runs in the background. Call sandbox_end_record_screen when done to
stop and retrieve the video. Only one recording can be active at a time.

name: sandbox_end_record_screen
description: Stop recording and return video as base64.
detailed_description: Stop the active screen recording and return the video file as a base64-encoded
string (mp4 format). Must have called sandbox_record_screen first.

name: list_branches
detailed_description: List all git branches in the project repository. Use to see existing branches
before creating a new one, or to find the branch associated with a task.

name: view_diff
detailed_description: Show the git diff between two refs (defaults: base=main, head=HEAD). Use to
review changes before creating a PR, verify your own work, or understand what
changed on a branch.

name: create_task
detailed_description: Create a new task on the project kanban board (manager only). Set critical
(1–10) and urgent (1–10) to help prioritize. Tasks start in 'backlog' status.
After creation, use assign_task to hand it to an engineer.

name: assign_task
detailed_description: Assign an existing task to an agent by their UUID (manager only). The agent will
be able to see and work on the task. Use list_agents to find available engineer
IDs and list_tasks to find unassigned tasks.

name: update_task_status
detailed_description: Update a task's status (manager or engineer). Valid statuses: backlog,
in_progress, review, done, aborted. Engineers can only update tasks assigned to
them; managers can update any task.

name: abort_task
detailed_description: Abort your currently assigned task (engineer only). Sends a notification to the
task creator explaining the abort. Use when a task is blocked, infeasible, or
needs to be re-scoped. Always provide a clear reason.

name: interrupt_agent
detailed_description: Interrupt another agent's current session, forcing it to stop and re-evaluate
(manager/CTO only). Use when an agent is stuck, working on the wrong thing, or
when priorities have changed and it needs immediate redirection.

name: spawn_engineer
detailed_description: Create a new engineer agent for the project (manager/CTO only). The engineer is
started immediately and ready to receive messages or task assignments. Use when
more parallelism is needed or when specialized work requires a dedicated
engineer. Maximum 5 engineers per project.
- display_name: A human-readable label (e.g. 'Backend-Engineer', 'Engineer-3').
- seniority: Engineer seniority level (optional). Must be one of
'junior', 'intermediate', or 'senior'.
Model selection is controlled by user configuration only
(engineer_junior_model / engineer_intermediate_model / engineer_senior_model).

name: list_agents
detailed_description: List all agents in the project with their UUIDs, display names, roles, and
current status (manager/CTO only). Use to find agent UUIDs for messaging or task
assignment, or to review the current team composition.

name: describe_tool
description: Get detailed description and usage guide for a tool.
detailed_description: Look up detailed usage information for any tool by name. Returns the tool's
purpose, full parameter documentation (types, required/optional), and which
roles can use it. Call with no tool_name to list all tools available to you.

tool_name.description: Name of the tool to describe. Omit to list all available tools.

name: create_branch
detailed_description: Create a new git branch from main (CTO/engineer only). Use before starting work
on a task to isolate changes. Branch names should be descriptive (e.g.
'feat/user-auth', 'fix/login-bug').

name: create_pull_request
description: Create pull request from current branch.
detailed_description: Create a pull request from the current branch to main (CTO/engineer only). Call
after completing work on a feature branch to request code review. Provide a
clear title and description summarizing the changes.
